agent_notes: Haringey can produce a strong but not 30-venue PAINTS longlist; strict Books & Coffee and current-exhibition gallery supply is the binding constraint.
Creative Walks only use PAINTS-approved Alexandra Park, Highgate Wood and Queen's Wood.
Coordinates are postcode/address centroids cross-checked with public postcode data where venue-specific lat/long was not published; host should verify exact door pins before publishing.

venues.neighbourhood_station: Crouch End; nearest rail/tube: Hornsey / Finsbury Park bus
venues.opening_hours: Tue-Sat 18:00-00:00 per official/current listings; holiday handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Official booking page says some tables are kept back for walk-ins; group of 8-12 should call ahead.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely for early evening only; intimate bar, host should call before publishing.
venues.capacity_notes: Small cocktail bar with table service and sharing plates; avoid late peak.
venues.why_fits_paints: A polished neighbourhood cocktail bar with enough craft detail to support show-and-tell without feeling corporate. The drinks programme has press validation and the room is intimate enough for strangers to talk. Crouch End gives the list a north-London counterweight.
venues.anecdote_story: Opened by Alan Sherwood with Max and Noel Venning, bringing serious cocktail pedigree to Crouch End.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Cocktails, wine, beer and small/sharing plates.
venues.safety_notes: Busy Crouch End parade; late return relies on buses or pre-planned taxi/rail connection.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 8-10 min walk via Middle Lane - host to verify on Google Maps.
venues.risks_watchouts: Small venue; walk-in seats may not hold 8-12 at peak.

venues.opening_hours: Wed 17:00-22:00 and other weekly hours listed on official site; full current week and holiday handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: UNVERIFIED - host must confirm; appears to operate as neighbourhood wine bar/shop.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely tight; call for early-evening group fit.
venues.capacity_notes: Small local wine bar/shop from the Middle Lane Market team.
venues.best_time_slot: Wed/Thu early evening if current hours allow.
venues.why_fits_paints: A very local wine-bar texture in a less obvious residential pocket. The Middle Lane Market connection gives it a producer-led story. It is included as Tier B because walk-in/group tolerance needs direct confirmation.
venues.anecdote_story: Launched by the team behind Middle Lane Market, extending a Crouch End food-and-wine operator into Harringay.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Wine bar and shop; natural/independent wine focus inferred from operator sources.
venues.safety_notes: Residential street close to Harringay rail; plan dispersal after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 10 min walk - host to verify.
venues.risks_watchouts: Limited public operational detail and possible small room.

venues.opening_hours: Official shop site confirms trading and click-and-collect; bar hours and holiday handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: UNVERIFIED - host must confirm for 8-12; likely better as early-evening tasting or shop/bar hybrid.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Possible if pre-agreed; not a large-format bar.
venues.capacity_notes: Artisan cheese, natural wine, craft beer and pantry shop with wine-bar character.
venues.why_fits_paints: It has the right producer-led, tactile food-and-wine identity for a show-and-tell drinks session. The format can orbit cheese, wine labels and neighbourhood supply chains rather than generic cocktails. Stronger as a hosted micro-tasting than a pure walk-in.
venues.anecdote_story: A local food shop connecting people to producers, later spawning Bonne Route as a wine bar/shop.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Artisan cheese, natural wine, craft beer and pantry goods.
venues.safety_notes: Crouch End/Hornsey residential high street context; standard evening caution.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 8-10 min walk - host to verify.
venues.risks_watchouts: Walk-in and seating capacity need direct confirmation.

venues.opening_hours: Beer Guide/CAMRA list open from 16:30 Tue-Sun or Wed-Sun variants; official social channels should be checked for week-of changes.
venues.walk_in_policy: Taproom walk-in likely; group of 8-12 UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good candidate for a casual group if hours confirmed.
venues.capacity_notes: Brewery taproom on Myddleton Road; limited-hours risk.
venues.why_fits_paints: Czech lager made by Czech expats in a Bowes Park taproom is a clean, memorable story. The Myddleton Road setting gives it local-neighbourhood texture. It is less polished than Crouch End but stronger as a hosted taste-and-talk anchor.
venues.anecdote_story: Bohem describes itself as traditional Bohemian lagers brewed by Czech expats in London; the taproom was relaunched by the Mayor of Haringey in 2017.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Bohemian-style lagers and rotating taproom beers.
venues.safety_notes: Quiet Bowes Park high street; use Bowes Park rail / Bounds Green tube routes.
venues.backup_venue: Myddleton Road Market pubs/cafes only during market days; nearest same-category fallback is The Goodness Brewing by short bus/taxi.
venues.backup_walk_time: No qualifying same-category backup verified within 10 min; demoted risk considered but retained Tier A for strong taproom fit.
venues.risks_watchouts: Hours vary by source; host must verify current taproom calendar.

venues.opening_hours: Official site says hours change weekly; Instagram/Beer Guide show Fri-Sun taproom pattern. Host must confirm current week.
venues.walk_in_policy: Likely walk-in taproom; group of 8-12 UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Beer garden improves group suitability; hours are the risk.
venues.capacity_notes: Taproom with giant beer garden and food options.
venues.why_fits_paints: A working independent brewery gives people something tangible to discuss. The yard setting is more memorable than a generic pub. Weekly-changing hours cap the score.
venues.anecdote_story: Founded in Wood Green in 2018 by local friends and now led by head brewer Josh Billington.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Independent beer, vegan/gluten-free options, beer garden.
venues.safety_notes: Industrial/yard setting near Wood Green; use daylight or early evening dispersal.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 2 min walk.
venues.risks_watchouts: Weekly hours and private/music events can change the feel.

venues.opening_hours: Official site: Monday from 16:00; Tue-Sun from midday; kitchen until 21:30, Sunday 21:00.
venues.walk_in_policy: Pub walk-ins likely accepted; group of 8-12 should call ahead.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good if early; covered terrace can help.
venues.capacity_notes: Large corner pub with indoor room and covered terrace.
venues.why_fits_paints: The Palm has the restored-local-pub quality PAINTS likes: handsome but not stiff. It serves Tottenham rather than a predictable north-west cluster. Press notes the terrace and informal food-drink fit.
venues.anecdote_story: Former Truman pub/Lord Palmerston dating back to the 1850s according to beer-guide sources.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Craft on draft, wine, pub food/tapas-style plates.
venues.safety_notes: Philip Lane is urban and busy; keep event ending before late-night dispersal.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 10 min walk - host to verify.
venues.risks_watchouts: Match days and peak pub hours may reduce conversational quality.

venues.opening_hours: Haringey/CAMRA: Tue-Thu 16:00-23:00, Fri 14:00-00:00, Sat 12:00-00:00, Sun 12:00-22:30; Monday closed.
venues.walk_in_policy: Community pub; booking available but walk-in likely. Group of 8-12 should call ahead.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good early, with seating and garden; avoid Spurs match surges.
venues.capacity_notes: Community-owned pub seating about 60 with small covered garden per venue material.
venues.why_fits_paints: North London's first community-owned pub gives the host an immediate story. It is local, resilient and specific to Tottenham. The Bruce Castle proximity also gives an easy cultural pre/post walk.
venues.anecdote_story: Saved by a community share offer and operating as a community-owned pub since 2015.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Real ale, local Redemption beer, guest ales and pop-up food.
venues.safety_notes: Check Tottenham Hotspur match calendar; route groups via well-lit Church Road/Bruce Grove.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 10-12 min walk; just outside ideal radius, host to verify.
venues.risks_watchouts: Match-day crowding and backup slightly beyond ideal 10 minutes.

venues.opening_hours: Current hours UNVERIFIED - host must confirm with venue.
venues.walk_in_policy: Pub walk-ins likely; group of 8-12 should call ahead.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good room scale; can get noisy.
venues.capacity_notes: Large historic pub with ornate interior.
venues.why_fits_paints: The Grade II* Victorian interior is highly photographable and story-rich. It is less category-specific than a wine bar, so it sits at Tier B. Good fallback for Crouch End drinks when Little Mercies is full.
venues.anecdote_story: Originally the Queen's Hotel, built around 1898-1902 with Art Nouveau detailing.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Pub drinks, cask ale/beer and food.
venues.safety_notes: Central Crouch End; standard late-evening transit planning.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 2 min walk.
venues.risks_watchouts: Part of a pub group rather than fully independent; less PAINTS-specific drinks story.

venues.opening_hours: Every Sunday 10:00-15:00 per CCFM; location alternates between Alexandra Palace Park and Campsbourne School.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market; no reservation required.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Excellent for browsing in pairs and regrouping.
venues.capacity_notes: Outdoor farmers' market; weather-dependent.
venues.why_fits_paints: Producer-led food, park views and the Palace backdrop make it Haringey's strongest market day. It has a clear weekly rhythm and enough sensory material for conversation. The Palace/Park history adds a satisfying cultural layer.
venues.anecdote_story: Founded by City & Country Farmers' Markets; the park and Palace are the historic 'People's Palace' landscape.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Farm produce, bread, cakes, meat, cheese, eggs, raw milk, preserves, hot food and crafts depending on week.
venues.safety_notes: Use daytime only; Alexandra Park is PAINTS Premium-approved for walks and suitable as a market anchor.
venues.backup_venue: Muswell Hill Creatives market when scheduled
venues.backup_walk_time: About 15 min by bus/walk; market backup radius relaxed to 15 min transit.
venues.risks_watchouts: Check exact weekly location before publishing.

venues.opening_hours: Monthly first Sunday pattern; official site lists 2026 dates and 11:00-16:00 market times.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good; street market format supports flexible pacing.
venues.capacity_notes: Fresh producers, hot food, crafts, homewares, vintage, art and drinks.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is a neighbourhood market with real maker and vintage texture, not a generic food court. Myddleton Road has enough independent frontage to make the event feel like a small discovery. It pairs naturally with Bohem Tap Room.
venues.anecdote_story: Market activity is part of Haringey's strategy to strengthen distinctive local high streets.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Art, jewellery, crafts, artisan bread, cakes, hot food, refill truck and rotating stalls.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime street market; use Bowes Park station/Bounds Green routes.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 15 min by bus/taxi; acceptable market backup transit radius.
venues.risks_watchouts: Monthly only; host must check exact date and stall list.

venues.opening_hours: Quarterly/seasonal makers fairs; recent listings show 10:30-17:00. Host must verify next date.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access makers fair.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good when scheduled; browse-and-regroup format.
venues.capacity_notes: Outdoor makers fair around St James Square.
venues.why_fits_paints: A local collective of artists, designers and makers is directly aligned with PAINTS' craft-first taste. Muswell Hill's village high street gives easy coffee/bookshop pairings. The only weakness is seasonality.
venues.anecdote_story: Muswell Hill Creatives is a collective of local artists, designers and makers promoting quality craftsmanship.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Ceramics, jewellery, leatherwork, prints, candles, paper goods and other maker stalls.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime high-street square with good footfall.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 15 min by bus/walk; acceptable for market backup.
venues.risks_watchouts: Not weekly; must verify date and weather plan.

venues.opening_hours: Haringey strategy notes monthly relaunch from June 2025; exact 2026 schedule UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market when operating.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good if stall count is strong; risk of thin trading.
venues.capacity_notes: Outdoor market/festival format on Tottenham Green.
venues.why_fits_paints: Tottenham Green is geographically valuable and community-facing. It is Tier B because recent public evidence suggests variable stall strength and schedule uncertainty. Best used when tied to a confirmed festival/market programme.
venues.anecdote_story: Started as a local market on Tottenham Green and remains part of Haringey's market-development strategy.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Street food, drink, crafts, vintage and local produce when active.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime only; use Seven Sisters/Tottenham Hale routes and avoid late dispersal.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 15 min walk/transit.
venues.risks_watchouts: Schedule and stall quality need host verification.

venues.opening_hours: Instagram says first Saturday monthly, 10:00 start; full schedule UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable if the current stall list is strong.
venues.capacity_notes: Community market at Harris Primary Academy / Philip Lane area.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is hyperlocal and community-led, useful as a Tottenham/West Green counterweight. It lacks the proven scale of Ally Pally or Myddleton Road, so it sits at Tier B. Use only when the posted stall list is compelling.
venues.anecdote_story: A first-Saturday market serving West Green/Tottenham.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Local stalls; exact mix varies.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime only; plan station routes.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 15 min walk/transit.
venues.risks_watchouts: Thin public evidence and variable stall mix.

venues.venue_name: Alexandra Park Creative Walk: People's Palace, BBC Terrace and Grove
venues.opening_hours: Park/car parks have published opening windows; route is daytime only. Palace event-day changes must be checked.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access park route.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Excellent; wide paths and multiple regrouping points.
venues.capacity_notes: Outdoor 2-3 hour walk; accessible route planning required for slopes.
venues.best_time_slot: Sunday 10:30 with farmers' market, or weekday 14:00.
venues.why_fits_paints: Alexandra Park is on PAINTS' Premium Launch safety list and has one of the best story-to-view ratios in Haringey. Stops can include the Palace south terrace, BBC/television history, the old racecourse landscape and The Grove. It is the borough's clearest Tier A creative walk.
venues.anecdote_story: The 'People's Palace' opened in the 1870s and later became central to early BBC television broadcasting.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Views, palace architecture, BBC history, parkland and optional farmers' market.
venues.safety_notes: PAINTS Premium Launch park; daytime recommended, avoid isolated paths after dark.
venues.backup_venue: The Grove Cafe / Alexandra Palace pub depending on start point
venues.backup_walk_time: Within 5 min of route start if starting at The Grove or Palace.
venues.risks_watchouts: Steep gradients, weather exposure and event-day crowds.

venues.venue_name: Highgate Wood Creative Walk: Ancient Woodland Loop
venues.opening_hours: Dawn until dusk/daylight hours per City of London/GoParks sources.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access park/wood route.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good in daylight; paths can narrow/muddy.
venues.capacity_notes: Ancient woodland loop with cafe/toilets nearby.
venues.best_time_slot: Late morning or early afternoon; avoid dusk.
venues.why_fits_paints: Highgate Wood is on the PAINTS Safety-First Shortlist and offers a quieter, more tactile walk than Alexandra Park. Stops can cover ancient woodland indicators, hornbeam/oak ecology, Roman kiln archaeology and the transition to Queen's Wood. It is strong but gets a safety caveat for woodland dusk conditions.
venues.anecdote_story: Part of the ancient Forest of Middlesex and now managed by the City of London.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Ancient woodland, wildlife, tree species, Roman kiln/history interpretation.
venues.safety_notes: PAINTS Safety-First Shortlist; daylight only, avoid isolated paths in poor light.
venues.backup_venue: Pavilion Cafe / nearby Muswell Hill cafes
venues.backup_walk_time: Within 5 min of main entrances if pre-selected; host must verify current cafe hours.
venues.risks_watchouts: Muddy paths, winter darkness and cafe-hours uncertainty.

venues.venue_name: Queen's Wood Creative Walk: Woodland Cafe, Pond and Ancient Trees
venues.opening_hours: GoParks lists always open; practical PAINTS use should be daylight only.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access woodland route.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good in daylight; smaller paths than Alexandra Park.
venues.capacity_notes: Woodland route with cafe and benches; weather affects comfort.
venues.why_fits_paints: Queen's Wood is on the PAINTS Safety-First Shortlist and has a tucked-away, storybook quality. It is ideal for a quieter creative walk with sketching/observation prompts. Pairing with Highgate Wood gives a richer ancient-woodland narrative.
venues.anecdote_story: One of Haringey's ancient woods and opened as a public wood in the late nineteenth century.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Ancient woodland, pond, cafe/lodge setting, seasonal flora.
venues.safety_notes: PAINTS Safety-First Shortlist; no dusk or after-dark routing.
venues.backup_walk_time: At/within 5 min of start if using cafe/lodge start.
venues.risks_watchouts: Cafe hours and woodland conditions must be checked day-of.

venues.opening_hours: Museum/event hours vary by exhibition and programme; host must confirm current opening and bank-holiday handling.
venues.walk_in_policy: Free museum/event entry often walk-in; timed/limited events must be confirmed.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for daytime self-guided visit if open.
venues.capacity_notes: Museum/archive rooms in historic building; event rooms vary.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is Haringey's strongest museum pick: local history, archive depth and current exhibition programming. The building and postal-history/Rowland Hill material give hosts more than one story route. It anchors Tottenham without relying on food or nightlife.
venues.anecdote_story: Permanent galleries include Haringey postal heritage and Sir Rowland Hill's penny-post connection.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Current exhibitions plus postal history, collecting communities and local-history collections.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime visit recommended; pair with Antwerp Arms only if routes and timing are host-checked.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 15-20 min walk or short bus; no qualifying gallery backup within 10 min, so operational caveat applies.
venues.risks_watchouts: Current exhibition press is local/official rather than major art press; backup exceeds ideal radius.

venues.opening_hours: Gallery hours and current exhibition dates must be confirmed; recent site shows exhibitions through June 2026 and Instagram references current show.
venues.walk_in_policy: Commercial gallery walk-in likely during open hours; group of 8-12 UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Small gallery; split viewing likely.
venues.capacity_notes: Print studio/publisher/gallery; best for short focused viewing.
venues.best_time_slot: Thu-Sat afternoon if current show is open.
venues.why_fits_paints: Jealous is a real print publisher/gallery, giving a craft-process angle absent from many local art spaces. It is Tier B because current exhibition and group walk-in details need week-of confirmation. Strong Crouch End pairing with coffee/drinks nearby.
venues.anecdote_story: Jealous operates as a print studio, publisher and gallery with artist collaborations.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Contemporary prints and gallery exhibitions.
venues.safety_notes: Central Crouch End; good daytime/early evening context.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 8-10 min walk.
venues.risks_watchouts: Exhibition may change or be between shows.

venues.opening_hours: Library/gallery hours vary; host must confirm current exhibition and opening.
venues.walk_in_policy: Library gallery walk-in likely during public hours; group suitability UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for small-group viewing if exhibition open.
venues.capacity_notes: Council/library gallery with Original Gallery and Promenade Gallery spaces.
venues.best_time_slot: Saturday afternoon or weekday late afternoon if open.
venues.why_fits_paints: It is a practical Crouch End gallery backup with emerging/local artists. The library setting is less glamorous but accessible and community-rooted. It sits below Tier A because current show/press evidence is light.
venues.anecdote_story: Hornsey Library includes permanent artworks and commercial gallery spaces for established and emerging artists.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Rotating exhibitions, local artists and library permanent artworks.
venues.safety_notes: Central Crouch End; daytime/early evening recommended.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 8-10 min walk.
venues.risks_watchouts: Current exhibition details must be checked before listing.

venues.opening_hours: Programme-led; Music Glue lists July 2026 events. Gallery/daytime access UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Ticketed events and private-event closures occur; walk-in gallery use UNVERIFIED.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for ticketed cultural night; less certain for gallery-day format.
venues.capacity_notes: Arts venue/vegan restaurant/bar connected to Collage Arts.
venues.best_time_slot: Ticketed evening or Sunday roast/cultural programme when open.
venues.why_fits_paints: Karamel is a real Wood Green arts ecosystem node rather than a polished gallery. It is useful for a mixed arts day or as weather backup near Goodness. Tier B because current visual-art exhibition access is not consistently published.
venues.anecdote_story: Collage Arts has supported Wood Green's alternative arts scene, with Karamel and nearby creative spaces in the N22 corridor.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Music, performance, community arts, occasional exhibitions and vegan food/bar.
venues.safety_notes: Wood Green evening route should be planned; avoid private-event nights.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 2 min walk.
venues.risks_watchouts: Not always a gallery; programme may be music/private hire.

venues.opening_hours: Programme-led; current events listed on venue/Haringey/TicketSource pages. Gallery/open walk-in status UNVERIFIED.
venues.walk_in_policy: Ticketed events; walk-in gallery/cafe use UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for ticketed performance/cultural event; weaker for gallery-day format.
venues.capacity_notes: Multi-arts centre with theatre and cafe.
venues.best_time_slot: Ticketed evening or daytime cafe/cultural event if confirmed.
venues.why_fits_paints: The David Adjaye-designed centre has strong cultural and architectural story value for Tottenham. It is Tier B because it does not reliably meet the paintings/sculpture/current-exhibition requirement from public sources. Best as a cultural alternative rather than a pure gallery day.
venues.anecdote_story: Opened in 2007 as a purpose-built multi-arts centre connected to Bernie Grant's legacy.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Theatre, music, events, cafe and occasional visual/cultural programming.
venues.safety_notes: Tottenham Green/Seven Sisters urban route; plan arrival and departure.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 15-20 min walk or short bus; outside strict 10-min gallery backup.
venues.risks_watchouts: Category fit is borderline unless a visual-art exhibition is confirmed.

venues.opening_hours: Instagram/Bookshop Crawl list Mon-Sat 09:30-17:30 and Sun 11:00-16:00; host must confirm holiday hours.
venues.walk_in_policy: Bookshop/cafe walk-ins likely; group of 8-12 should call ahead because events/classes use the space.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good off-peak; children/family programming may make quiet reading harder at times.
venues.capacity_notes: Specialist children's bookshop, cafe and events/theatre space; Skiddle lists capacity 40.
venues.best_time_slot: Weekday late morning or early afternoon outside classes.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is the borough's cleanest Books & Coffee fit: books, cafe and event identity all in one place. It has a warm, tactile Crouch End character and a genuine author/illustrator ecosystem. Best for a playful, intergenerational literary prompt rather than silent reading.
venues.anecdote_story: A specialist children's bookshop/cafe/events space hosting authors, illustrators, classes and theatre.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Children's books, cafe drinks, events, author/illustrator programming and theatre.
venues.safety_notes: Central Crouch End; daytime suitable.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 25 min walk / short bus; no qualifying Books & Coffee backup within 10 min, operational caveat applies.
venues.risks_watchouts: Event schedule may limit quiet seating; backup outside strict radius.

venues.opening_hours: Official site says open seven days; exact daily hours and holiday handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Bookshop walk-ins likely; events may be ticketed. Group of 8-12 UNVERIFIED.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for events/book groups; coffee provision is the weak point.
venues.capacity_notes: Cooperative bookshop and small arts venue with regular talks, book launches and groups.
venues.best_time_slot: Afternoon browsing or a listed book/event evening.
venues.why_fits_paints: The cooperative/community arts identity is excellent for PAINTS. It is Tier B because public sources confirm books and event/bar activity more clearly than coffee. Use for a literary event or reading-group format rather than a classic cafe sit.
venues.anecdote_story: Volunteer/cooperative bookshop run as a local arts venue with writing, art, repair, music and discussion groups.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Books, groups/events and weekend/event bar activity.
venues.safety_notes: Turnpike Lane/Wood Green route should be daytime or early evening with clear station plan.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 15-18 min walk; no strict Books & Coffee backup within 10 min.
venues.risks_watchouts: Coffee/cafe criterion is not fully evidenced; host must confirm beverage offer.

venues.opening_hours: Hours vary; social/press confirm current operation, host must verify exact hours.
venues.walk_in_policy: Bookshop walk-ins likely; group seating/coffee UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Better for browsing and a short discussion than a seated 2-hour cafe session.
venues.capacity_notes: Secondhand bookshop in former funeral parlour with events/parlour space.
venues.best_time_slot: Weekend late morning if open and not hosting children's activities.
venues.why_fits_paints: The ex-funeral-parlour story and secondhand shelves are very PAINTS. It is capped at Tier B because coffee and 8-12 seating are not reliably evidenced. Strong as part of a Muswell Hill bookshop crawl paired with an independent cafe.
venues.anecdote_story: Opened in 2025/2026 in a former funeral parlour by Chris and Katrina Masson, with secondhand books, art, records and events.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Secondhand books, art, records and literary/children's activities.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime Muswell Hill high street; good footfall.
venues.backup_venue: The Children's Bookshop / House of Books cluster
venues.backup_walk_time: Within 5-10 min walk, but coffee criterion remains weak.
venues.risks_watchouts: Coffee offer and seating are not sufficiently verified for strict category fit.

rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Closed according to official venue page; do not source.
Explicitly removed on PAINTS v6 safety list; cannot be used as route or backup.
Not on PAINTS-approved Creative Walk safety list, despite local interest; must be flagged for team review rather than sourced.
Excellent independent children's bookshop with events, but no on-site cafe or literal next-door coffee partner was verified.
Strong independent bookshop, but coffee/on-site seating requirement not verified; better as bookshop-crawl stop than standalone Books & Coffee venue.
Good secondhand/charity bookshop and vinyl selection, but no coffee/cafe fit; below PAINTS category gate.
Pleasant pub but more templated pub-group feel and weaker owner/story signal than The Queens, Little Mercies or Middle Lane Market.

rejected_candidates.venue_name: Alexandra Palace as Museum & Gallery Day
